---
title: "Mesfin Genie"
page-layout: full
description: "Mesfin Genie is a Senior Lecturer in Health Economics at the University of Newcastle, specialising in health preferences, choice modelling, behavioural decision science and public health policy."
image: assets/mesfin-genie-profile.webp
---

::: {.hero}
::: {.page-shell .hero-grid}
::: {.profile-photo-wrap}
![](assets/mesfin-genie-profile.webp){.profile-photo alt="Mesfin Genie"}
:::

::: {.hero-content}
<div class="eyebrow">Health economist · choice modeller</div>

# Mesfin Genie

<div class="hero-role">Senior Lecturer in Health Economics</div>
<div class="hero-affiliation">Newcastle Business School · The University of Newcastle, Australia</div>

<div class="hero-copy">
I study how patients, citizens and decision-makers make trade-offs in health and public policy. My research combines health economics, discrete choice experiments, behavioural decision science, eye tracking and applied econometrics.

I develop methods for understanding how people process information in complex choices and translate empirical evidence into practical tools for policy and programme design.
</div>

<div class="hero-actions">
<a class="button-primary" href="/publications/">Publications</a>
<a class="button-secondary" href="/research/">Research</a>
<a class="button-secondary" href="/cv/">Curriculum vitae</a>
</div>

<div class="social-row" aria-label="Academic and professional profiles">
<a class="social-link" href="https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=v2SXM_kAAAAJ&hl=en" title="Google Scholar" aria-label="Google Scholar"><i class="ai ai-google-scholar"></i></a>
<a class="social-link" href="https://orcid.org/0000-0002-1744-4666" title="ORCID" aria-label="ORCID"><i class="ai ai-orcid"></i></a>
<a class="social-link" href="https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Mesfin-Genie" title="ResearchGate" aria-label="ResearchGate"><i class="ai ai-researchgate"></i></a>
<a class="social-link" href="https://au.linkedin.com/in/mesfin-genie-ph-d-65634216" title="LinkedIn" aria-label="LinkedIn"><i class="bi bi-linkedin"></i></a>
<a class="social-link" href="https://github.com/DrGenie" title="GitHub" aria-label="GitHub"><i class="bi bi-github"></i></a>
<a class="social-link" href="https://x.com/mesfin_genie" title="X" aria-label="X"><i class="bi bi-twitter"></i></a>
<a class="social-link" href="https://www.newcastle.edu.au/profile/mesfin-genie" title="University profile" aria-label="University profile"><i class="bi bi-building"></i></a>
<a class="social-link" href="mailto:mesfin.genie@newcastle.edu.au" title="Email" aria-label="Email"><i class="bi bi-envelope"></i></a>
</div>
:::
:::
:::

::: {.section}
::: {.page-shell}
::: {.section-heading}
<div class="section-rule"></div>

## Research

My work centres on three connected areas.
:::

::: {.grid-3}
::: {.theme-card}
### Health preferences and choice modelling

Discrete choice experiments, preference heterogeneity, equity, value assessment and patient-centred decision-making.
:::

::: {.theme-card}
### Behaviour in complex decisions

Attention, information processing, numeracy, feedback, attribute ordering and eye-tracking evidence within stated-preference studies.
:::

::: {.theme-card}
### Public health policy and evaluation

Vaccination policy, mandates, telehealth, preventive care and the economic evaluation of health and public programmes.
:::
:::
:::
:::

::: {.section .alt}
::: {.page-shell}
::: {.section-heading}
<div class="section-rule"></div>

## Recent publications
:::

<div class="publication-list">
<div class="publication-item">
<div class="publication-year">2026</div>
<div>
<div class="publication-title">Fit for purpose? Assessing the robustness of discrete choice experiment designs in the context of evolving health technologies</div>
<div class="publication-authors">Mesfin G. Genie, Verity Watson, Michaël Schwarzinger and Stéphane Luchini</div>
<div class="publication-meta"><em>Social Science & Medicine</em> · <span class="status">Published</span></div>
<div class="publication-links"><a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.socscimed.2026.119463">DOI</a></div>
</div>
</div>

<div class="publication-item">
<div class="publication-year">2026</div>
<div>
<div class="publication-title">Guidance or misdirection? Unpacking the role of feedback in health preference assessments</div>
<div class="publication-authors">Mesfin G. Genie, Shelby D. Reed and Semra Ozdemir</div>
<div class="publication-meta"><em>Health Economics</em> · <span class="status">Published</span></div>
<div class="publication-links"><a href="https://doi.org/10.1002/hec.70093">DOI</a></div>
</div>
</div>

<div class="publication-item">
<div class="publication-year">2026</div>
<div>
<div class="publication-title">Priority for self or others? Incorporating equity considerations in a preference-based health value assessment</div>
<div class="publication-authors">Mesfin G. Genie and Surachat Ngorsuraches</div>
<div class="publication-meta"><em>Value in Health</em> · <span class="status">Published</span></div>
<div class="publication-links"><a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jval.2025.10.011">DOI</a></div>
</div>
</div>

<div class="publication-item">
<div class="publication-year">2026</div>
<div>
<div class="publication-title">Feeling lonely? Preferences for support programmes to reduce loneliness among older adults in Australia</div>
<div class="publication-authors">Mesfin G. Genie, Allison W. Boyes and Francesco Paolucci</div>
<div class="publication-meta"><em>Health Policy</em> · <span class="status">Published</span></div>
<div class="publication-links"><a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.healthpol.2026.105587">DOI</a></div>
</div>
</div>
</div>

<p style="margin-top:1.4rem"><a class="button-secondary" href="/publications/">View all publications</a></p>
:::
:::

::: {.section}
::: {.page-shell}
::: {.section-heading}
<div class="section-rule"></div>

## Digital tools
:::

::: {.grid-2}
::: {.tool-card}
<div class="tool-kicker">Vaccine policy</div>

### eMANDEVA Decision Aid

An interactive decision-support application for evaluating vaccine-mandate designs, expected public support, health benefits and policy trade-offs.

<a class="tool-link" data-open-new="true" href="https://mesfingenie.com/eMANDEVA-DecisionAid-V18/">Open eMANDEVA <i class="bi bi-arrow-up-right"></i></a>
:::

::: {.tool-card}
<div class="tool-kicker">Economic evaluation</div>

### Farming BCA Tool

A practical benefit–cost analysis platform for assessing farm-trial and soil-management interventions under alternative assumptions and sensitivity scenarios.

<a class="tool-link" data-open-new="true" href="https://mesfingenie.com/farming-bca-tool-v18/">Open Farming BCA Tool <i class="bi bi-arrow-up-right"></i></a>
:::
:::
:::
:::
